contraindications: Use caution with prescription medications and chronic conditions; monitor for intolerance.
safetyNotes: Adverse effects can include dizziness, nausea/vomiting, diarrhea, and muscle pain; pregnancy/lactation safety is uncertain. Some purported hawthorn-root/tejocote products may be substituted with toxic yellow oleander, so product identity matters.

sources.title: FDA warning about tejocote root supplements substituted with toxic yellow oleander

description: A flowering shrub/tree in the rose family occurring in temperate regions; hawthorn is an umbrella term spanning multiple Crataegus species in commerce and traditional use.

interactions: unresolved beyond cautious medication review language
preparation: Typically sold as hawthorn supplements and tejocote root products; preparation notes should emphasize verified identity.
region: Hawthorn broadly occurs in temperate regions worldwide.
therapeuticUses: traditional support for mild digestive comfort
sideEffects: mild gastrointestinal upset (nausea, cramping, or loose stool)
allergic rash or itching in sensitive individuals
summary: Hawthorn is promoted for heart/blood-vessel health and other uses; evidence in heart-failure studies is conflicting and insufficient for other cardiovascular endpoints. A major practical safety concern is product substitution/adulteration: some products labeled as tejocote root were adulterated with toxic yellow oleander.
